arts.ttl: Tottenham are brilliant against the big boys, but meek against the minnows - five reasons why Spurs are so consistently inconsistent, writes JAMES SHARPE
arts.sum: THE SHARPE END: Trying to make sense of Tottenham is, and always has been, an impossible task. Why can Tottenham beat the big clubs but get turned over by those they should beat?

arts.ttl: Tottenham v Chelsea: Best bets for all Sunday’s Premier League games
arts.sum: Our betting expert with punts for both the day’s London derbies, with Arsenal's trip to Fulham, Brighton at Leicester and Ipswich-Bournemouth PLUS a La Liga shout

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca insists Chelsea aren't favourites for London derby against Tottenham as the manager continues to play down the Blues' chances this season
arts.sum: Ange Postecoglou's team find themselves in a tough moment, two weeks on from their 4-0 beating of Manchester City.

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca sends Chelsea squad warning after making example of Noni Madueke
arts.sum: TOTTENHAM vs CHELSEA: Enzo Maresca wants to get the best out of every Chelsea player at his disposal and insists his tough love will continue throughout the season as he bids to fire the big-spending Blues to success

arts.ttl: The Big Question: Are Chelsea genuine title contenders?
arts.sum: With Chelsea sitting in second place ahead of Sunday's London derby at Tottenham Hotspur, football writer Ben Bloom looks at whether they can push for the Premier League title. Unbeaten in their last six Premier League games and winning their most recent three with an aggregate score of 10-2, Chelsea have climbed above Arsenal and Man City into second place.

arts.ttl: Gary Neville slams Man United for 'being outrun every single week' and reveals the lesson they could learn from Chelsea as Rio Ferdinand brands defeat to Nottingham Forest a 'shambles'
arts.sum: Gary Neville has criticised Manchester United for 'being outrun every single week'. Ruben Amorim's side suffered a second successive defeat as they were beaten 3-2 at home to Nottingham Forest.

arts.ttl: Analysis: Five areas where Spurs v Chelsea can be won and lost
arts.sum: To prevent this tactic threatening them, Spurs must press outstandingly well to ensure Chelsea’s players do not have time to look up and make the perfect pass. Nicolas Jackson, with six goals in his last nine matches, has the speed to trouble the Spurs back line when passes are slipped beyond the last defender.

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca speaks out on Chelsea's Cole Palmer dependency
arts.sum: Chelsea manager Enzo Maresca addressed concerns that his side are overly dependent on Cole Palmer.

arts.ttl: Marcel Desailly 'set for court date amid ongoing paternity row over 10-year-old daughter he has refused to acknowledge' which could see the Chelsea legend - who is 'struggling financially' - forced to pay £4000-a-month in alimony
arts.sum: Desailly and Cosma Batista de Alcantara are due at the Paris judicial court on Tuesday amid an ongoing case to confirm whether he is the biological father of his ex-partner's daughter.

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca is right to be cautious but rebuilt Chelsea are in the title race | Jacob Steinberg
arts.sum: Italian’s tactical versatility has made the Blues contenders as they head to Spurs, even if he insists it is too soon Nobody takes Enzo Maresca seriously any more. The Italian keeps playing down Chelsea’s chances of muscling themselves into the title race, but the line is becoming harder to hold. Modesty, after all, does become less believable when analysing a team with the Premier League’s most productive attack, growing bite and control in midfield, an improving defence and a hunger that makes them a match for anyone. Maresca was not budging before Sunday’s trip to Tottenham. The message remains that Chelsea are ahead of expectations. Maresca’s position is that his young side are not ready to compete with Arsenal, Liverpool and, yes, Manchester City. He believes his players need to suffer more before they possess the mentality required to match the very best over an entire campaign. Continue reading...
